tex:
Roberto and his family fly from London to Los Angeles on a holiday.

(a) The flight takes 11 hours 15 minutes.
(i) (b) The flight leaves London at 15 40 local time.
The local time in Los Angeles is 8 hours behind the local time in London.
Work out the local time in Los Angeles that the plane arrives.

 : 

(c) The plane flies a total of 8760 km.
Calculate the average speed of the plane.

 km/h

(d) Roberto hires a car.
(i) (e) The cost of hiring a car is \$56 per day, plus a fixed cost of \$436.
Write down a formula for the cost, C dollars, of hiring a car for d days.

 =  d +

There are four colours of car.

(g) The family visit a national park which has an area of 4986 km$^2$.
(i) (h) Write 4986 correct to the nearest hundred.

(i) Write 4986 in standard form.

(j) A ticket for the park costs \$17.50 plus 8\% tax.
Calculate the amount of tax paid.

(k) The scale drawing shows the positions of two viewing points, A and B, in the park.
The scale is 1 centimetre represents 5 kilometres.

(i) (l) Work out the actual distance between point A and point B.

 km

(m) Point C is 20 km from point A on a bearing of 072$^\circ$.
On the scale drawing mark the position of point C.

tex:
(a) The bar chart shows the number of cars sold by a garage in each of six months.

(i) (b) In July, 11 cars were sold.
Complete the bar chart.

(c) How many more cars were sold in March than in May?

(d) These are the opening times of the garage.
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|}
\hline
Monday to Friday & 8.30 am to 5.30 pm \\
\hline
Saturday & 8.30 am to 1.00 pm \\
\hline
Sunday & Closed \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
Work out how many hours the garage is open in one week.

 h

(e) Mohammed works at the garage.
He works for 36 hours from Monday to Friday and for 2 hours on Saturday.
He is paid \$10.50 per hour from Monday to Friday.
On Saturday he is paid $1\frac{1}{2}$ times this rate.
Calculate how much Mohammed is paid for this week.

\$ 

(f) Viktor is saving to buy a car.
He invests \$8000 for 5 years at a rate of 2.4\% per year compound interest.
Calculate the value of Viktor's investment at the end of the 5 years.
Give your answer correct to the nearest dollar.

\$ 

(g) At the garage, Pierre, Luigi and Freda sell cars.
They share a bonus of \$12 000 in the ratio Pierre : Luigi : Freda = 8 : 4 : 3.
Calculate the amount they each receive.

Pierre \$  \\
Luigi \$  \\
Freda \$
texHtml:

Monday to Friday8.30 am to 5.30 pm
Saturday8.30 am to 1.00 pm
SundayClosed

He is paid \$10.50 per hour from Monday to Friday. On Saturday he is paid $1\frac{1}{2}$ times this rate. Calculate how much Mohammed is paid for this week. \$ (f) Viktor is saving to buy a car. He invests \$8000 for 5 years at a rate of 2.4\% per year compound interest. Calculate the value of Viktor's investment at the end of the 5 years. Give your answer correct to the nearest dollar. \$ (g) At the garage, Pierre, Luigi and Freda sell cars. They share a bonus of \$12 000 in the ratio Pierre : Luigi : Freda = 8 : 4 : 3. Calculate the amount they each receive. Pierre \$ \\ Luigi \$ \\ Freda \$

(a) A cuboid measures 4 cm by 2 cm by 2 cm.
(i) (b) On the 1 cm$^2$ grid, draw an accurate net of this cuboid.
One face has been drawn for you.

(c) Calculate the surface area of the cuboid.

 cm$^2$

(d) A factory makes 5000 of these cuboids.
25 of the cuboids are checked and 3 of these cuboids are faulty.
How many of the 5000 cuboids are expected to be faulty?

(e) The surface area of a cube is 294 cm$^2$.
Calculate the volume of the cube.

 cm$^3$

Complete the statement about the value of l.

23.5  l  24.5

(a) Jean asks 600 people to choose their favourite sport.
The pie chart shows some of this information.

(i) (b) Show that 100 people choose tennis.

(c) Work out how many people choose rugby.

(d) 125 people choose cricket and the rest choose swimming.
Complete the pie chart to show this information.

(e) One of the 600 people is picked at random.
Find the probability that this person chooses tennis or cricket.
Give your answer as a fraction in its simplest form.

(f) There are 80 people in a group.
\begin{tabular}{|l|}
\hline
H = \{people who play hockey\} \\
\hline
N = \{people who play netball\} \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

36 people play hockey. \\
53 people play netball. \\
8 people do not play hockey or netball.

Complete the Venn diagram.
texHtml:
(a) Jean asks 600 people to choose their favourite sport. The pie chart shows some of this information. (i) (b) Show that 100 people choose tennis. (c) Work out how many people choose rugby. (d) 125 people choose cricket and the rest choose swimming. Complete the pie chart to show this information. (e) One of the 600 people is picked at random. Find the probability that this person chooses tennis or cricket. Give your answer as a fraction in its simplest form. (f) There are 80 people in a group.
H = \{people who play hockey\}
N = \{people who play netball\}
36 people play hockey. \\ 53 people play netball. \\ 8 people do not play hockey or netball. Complete the Venn diagram.
